summary: WIN-058b r=0.96 source verified: r=0.96 appears on wins.html WIN-058 entry as slope-regression goodness-of-fit (slope locked at 1.67 nT/µGal); r=0.992 appears on home-page Exhibit A as raw L1A residual time-series cross-correlation. They are plausibly different statistics on the same Halloween-storm data, not necessarily a self-contradiction. Our prior text said "the same home page cites r=0.992" — factually incorrect since r=0.96 is on wins.html, not home. EXP-164 patches detail_verdict_text and detail_claim to: (1) correctly attribute each r-value to its source page and statistical label, (2) neutralize the ISS-987 defense by arguing the distinction does not save the claim — neither number is computed by repository code, the 1.67 slope is tautological (10.9/6.5 by construction), both values are expected from shared electromagnetic forcing during a G5 storm, and WIN-058 is itself marked PENDING INDEPENDENT VERIFICATION on the dome site. Two patch blocks in data/wins.json for win 058b (detail_verdict_text + detail_claim).
